summary: FlexIT auto-payment trigger — client signs the contract, Stripe payment link fires automatically, no manual touchpoint

new: ⚡ SignWell webhook handler that automatically generates and emails a Stripe payment link the moment a client signs a FlexIT contract. Service desk and finance no longer need to touch Stripe to close a FlexIT deal — the rep clicks 'Send for Signature' once and the rest runs end-to-end

Auto-payment fires on CLIENT signature — not on full document completion. If countersignature is required, the client doesn't have to wait for the company countersign before receiving their payment link. Counter-signature happens in parallel

HubSpot deal note posted automatically when a FlexIT contract is signed: includes the SignWell document ID, the auto-generated Stripe checkout URL, and the email recipient. Visible on the deal timeline alongside Quote Notes

HubSpot pipeline stage automation — admin can configure 'Awaiting Payment' and 'Closed Won' stage IDs in Admin → Integrations → HubSpot. When configured, deals automatically move to 'Awaiting Payment' on contract signing. (When the client pays, the existing Stripe webhook will move them to 'Closed Won' — wired in this deploy)

Idempotency safeguard — SignWell may retry webhooks on transient failures, so we anchor each auto-payment to the SignWell document via a payment_id field. If a payment already exists, the webhook is a no-op

Webhook authentication via shared secret in the URL query string. SignWell does not publish HMAC signature verification, so we generate a 32-char hex secret in Admin → Integrations → SignWell and validate it on every incoming request
Auto-payment toggle in Admin → Integrations → Stripe — globally disable auto-trigger for testing or if you want a manual gate. Default is ON
FlexIT signature card now shows the live status of the auto-payment trail: 'Stripe payment link automatically generated' confirmation appears once the webhook has fired
improved: Stripe webhook now also handles the 'Closed Won' stage transition when configured — closes the loop on the deal: signed → awaiting payment → closed won, all without manual stage moves

summary: FlexIT direct-send for signature — packages quote + legal terms + signature block, sends through SignWell from the platform

new: ✏ New SignWell agreement card on FlexIT quotes (alongside SPT export and Stripe Prepayment cards). Sends a complete agreement — FlexIT order summary + Acceptance Terms / Exhibit A + signature block — directly from the platform without leaving Ferrum IQ. Replaces the manual SPT-then-SignWell workflow for FlexIT block deals

Server-side document assembly (buildSignatureDocument Netlify function) — the rep clicks Send, the function reads the live quote from Supabase plus the live legal HTML from pricing_settings, and that's exactly what gets uploaded to SignWell. Reps cannot tamper with document content from the browser

Send for Signature modal with two-pane layout: form on the left, live HTML preview on the right. Reps see exactly what the client will see before sending. "Print to PDF" button opens the rendered HTML in a new tab for download or PDF export

Per-document countersignature toggle with rule-based default — above the legal_countersign_threshold (default $5,000), countersign defaults ON; below, OFF. Rep can override either way. Reason for the default is shown next to the checkbox so the rep knows why

New Admin → Documents tab — edit the Acceptance Terms / Exhibit A HTML directly (with live preview), set the countersignature threshold, configure the default company signer name/title/email. When agreement links change, just paste new URLs into the editor — no deploy needed

Live status polling — the SignatureCard polls SignWell every 15s while a document is pending and updates the card in place when the client signs. Manual refresh and reminder buttons are also available

Quote-status gating — Send for Signature is disabled until the quote is in 'approved' status. Aligns with the existing review workflow: small deals can be self-approved by the rep, large deals go through Send for Review first
Test mode toggle in the modal — SignWell will mark the document as test (no real signature collected) for end-to-end verification before going live with a client
improved: Document tracking uses the existing quotes.inputs.signwellDocuments JSONB array pattern (matches LOA and IntlDialingWaiver) — no new tables, consistent shape across all signature document types
coming_soon: v3.5.18: SignWell webhook handler will fire automatically when the client signs — generates the Stripe payment link, emails the client, posts a HubSpot deal note, and updates the deal stage. Service desk and finance won't need to touch Stripe to close a FlexIT deal. The pricing_settings keys (flexit_auto_payment_after_sign, hubspot_stage_awaiting_payment, hubspot_stage_closed_won) are seeded now so admin can pre-configure them

summary: Stripe Integration card finally appears in Admin → Integrations (was wired into orphan file)

fixed: Stripe Integration card now actually shows up at /admin → Integrations tab. The v3.5.13 deploy added the StripeIntegration component to src/components/AdminPage.js, but App.js imports AdminPage from src/pages/AdminPage.js — so the modified file was never loaded by the app. This deploy adds StripeIntegration to the correct file
Removed the orphan src/components/AdminPage.js that was sitting unused in the repo. Nothing imported it; it was dead code from the v3.5.13 misplacement

summary: FlexIT — HubSpot deal description and totals now sync on save (was silently no-op)

fixed: FlexIT save now pushes the HubSpot Deal Description to the connected deal, matching IT Quote behavior. Previously the description was captured in local state and saved to Supabase, but never pushed to HubSpot — reps would update the description, save, and HubSpot would silently keep the old value
FlexIT now also calls updateDeal() on save to push contract value (= prepayment amount) and package name to HubSpot, parity with QuotePage

FlexIT URL-write-to-HubSpot now fires on EVERY save, not just on initial quote creation. Previously, edits to existing FlexIT quotes never re-wrote the URL field, so a deal would show the wrong URL if the quote was renamed or moved

summary: Stripe integration — collect Payment #1 prepayments by credit card with hosted Checkout

new: 💳 Stripe Prepayment card on every quote type (IT, Multi-Site, Bundle, FlexIT, Voice) — appears whenever the quote has a prepayment amount. Generate a hosted Stripe Checkout link, email it to the client, track status live

Card-only collection: Stripe handles credit cards exclusively. ACH/EFT remains free and external — clients pay via ferrumit.com/billing for no surcharge. Email template makes both options clear to the client

2% card processing fee shown as a separate line item on Stripe Checkout (labeled 'Card processing convenience fee') — transparent to the client, compliant with state-by-state surcharge rules. Rate is pulled from pricing_settings.payment_cc_surcharge so admin can adjust without a deploy
Hosted Stripe Checkout page (no card data ever touches Ferrum IQ) — PCI scope minimized to SAQ A. Statement descriptor configurable in Admin
Branded public receipt page at /pay/:sessionId — client lands here after paying, sees confirmation, payment details, and reference number. No login required
Live status sync — the StripePaymentCard polls Stripe every 8s while pending, updates instantly when payment lands. Webhook is the source of truth, polling is a UX sweetener
stripeWebhook Netlify function with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification (no SDK needed) — handles checkout.session.completed, expired, payment_intent.payment_failed, charge.refunded
stripeProxy Netlify function keeps the secret key server-side — mirrors the established signwellProxy / sptProxy / hubspot pattern

Admin → Integrations → Stripe: paste secret key, publishable key, webhook signing secret, statement descriptor; Test/Live mode auto-detected from key prefix; Test Connection button verifies API responds
Resend email, Copy link, Preview, and Cancel link buttons on every pending payment — reps have full control over the link lifecycle
Generate-new-link flow when a session expires (24h Stripe limit) — reuses the same payment row, increments resend_count for audit

stripe_payments table tracks every session: face amount, surcharge, total charged, mode (test/live), status, expiry, paid_at, payment_intent_id, charge_id, invoice_id, email send history, failure reasons, refund trail
improved: Payment Schedule Modal language and the Stripe Checkout description stay in lockstep — both pull from payment_cc_surcharge so the price the client sees on the quote matches exactly what Stripe charges

summary: SPT Create on FlexIT now actually generates the structured FlexIT proposal — fixes 'No proposal ID returned' error

improved: components/SPTConnect.js now accepts two new props: customCreate({ sptApiKey, name }) and quoteTypeLabel. customCreate lets a quote page hand SPTConnect a structured proposal builder (FlexIT passes one that calls createFlexITSPTProposal so the new SPT proposal includes the cover, billing summary, market-adjusted rate card, payment schedule, and acceptance terms — instead of an empty shell with just a name). quoteTypeLabel replaces the hardcoded 'Managed IT Services' suffix in the auto-generated proposal name; FlexIT passes 'On-Demand IT Support' so its proposals are named correctly

components/SPTConnect.js doCreate now extracts the new proposal id robustly — tries data.id, then data.proposal.id, data.proposal_id, data.data.id, data.data.proposal.id in turn. SPT's API response shape varies between endpoints and proxy versions; the previous code only checked data.id directly and would throw 'No proposal ID returned' even when the proposal had actually been created
fixed: FlexIT On-Demand quote — the 'New' / 'Create' button in the SPT Connect strip now generates the full FlexIT proposal in Smart Pricing Table. Previously SPTConnect ignored FlexIT's customCreate prop entirely (it wasn't declared in the component signature), so the click ran SPTConnect's built-in minimal create path which shipped just a name + recipient name. Worse: that built-in path was throwing 'No proposal ID returned' on some SPT responses where the id came back nested. With customCreate now wired AND robust id extraction, FlexIT reps get a fully structured proposal (cover, billing, rate card, payment schedule, acceptance terms) on first click

SPT Connect strip — auto-generated proposal name on FlexIT no longer reads 'Client — Managed IT Services'. Now uses the quoteTypeLabel prop so FlexIT proposals are named 'Client — On-Demand IT Support'. IT/Voice/Bundle/Multi-Site continue to default to 'Managed IT Services' since they don't pass quoteTypeLabel

summary: FlexIT Payment Schedule document now mirrors the on-screen Flex-Block-vs-Prepayment logic

improved: FlexIT Payment Schedule & Terms modal — when a flex block is selected, the payment table renders a single '#1 — Flex Block — Nhrs Pre-Purchased' row with the block price and 'Upon Agreement Signing — Block valid 12 months · refillable at this rate' due-date copy. When no flex block is selected, the row remains '#1 — Initial Prepayment' with the prepay amount, identical to the previous behavior. Same logic that v3.5.7 introduced on the on-screen Billing Summary, now reflected in the document the rep sends to the client

FlexIT Payment Schedule — the 'Setup Services Fee' section now becomes 'Flex Block Pre-Purchase' when applicable, with body copy describing the block as the engagement's initial commitment, its 12-month validity, and the 30-day rate-lock refill policy. Previously the Setup Services Fee paragraph still read 'A one-time setup, configuration, and installation fee of $X is due at the start...' on flex-block quotes — describing the prepay even when no prepay applied

Internal — flexHours, flexBlockPrice, and hasFlexBlock are now plumbed end-to-end from the FlexIT page state through FlexITDocumentsPanel into FlexITPaymentModal as explicit props. Previously the modal only knew about prepayAmount and was structurally unable to render the flex-block path

summary: HubSpot deal links now derive from deal id when the explicit URL is missing — fixes 'View Deal' looping back to the quote

new: lib/hubspot.js exports a new dealUrlFor(dealId) helper that builds a HubSpot deal record URL from just the deal id (HubSpot deal URLs follow the pattern https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/<portal>/record/0-3/<dealId>, so the explicit URL isn't strictly required when we have the id). The previous portal id was hardcoded inside createDeal — now it's a single HUBSPOT_PORTAL_ID constant at the top of the file
improved: lib/hubspot.js getDealFull(dealId) — wraps the proxy response and guarantees the returned full.dealUrl is populated, falling back to dealUrlFor(dealId) if the proxy omitted it. Previously the front-end would receive {dealUrl: undefined} from some deals and persist that as an empty string in hubspot_deal_url

components/HubSpotConnect.js View → button — href now reads `dealUrl || dealUrlFor(dealId)` so it always opens HubSpot, even on quotes whose hubspot_deal_url was never captured. Previously when dealUrl was empty the rendered <a href=''> reloaded the current page, which on a quote URL like /flexIT/<id> meant clicking 'View Deal' brought the rep right back to the quote instead of HubSpot

pages/QuotesPage.js HubSpot column — the View Deal link now renders whenever a quote has a hubspot_deal_id, even if hubspot_deal_url is null/empty in the row. The link's href falls back to dealUrlFor(q.hubspot_deal_id). Previously the column rendered '—' for any older FlexIT/IT/Voice/Bundle quote whose dealUrl wasn't captured at connect time, even though the deal id was right there in the row
fixed: FlexIT quote (and any other older quote where hubspot_deal_url ended up empty) — the 'View →' button in the HubSpot Connect strip no longer loops back to the quote itself. The proxy's get_deal_full response occasionally didn't include dealUrl, which the front-end stored as an empty string. With the saved URL empty, the View → link rendered as <a href=''> which the browser interprets as the current URL — reloading the same page. Now both the live View → button and the persisted DB column have synthesized fallbacks anchored on the deal id

Saved Quotes page — the HubSpot column no longer shows '—' for quotes that have a deal_id but were missing the deal_url. Existing affected quotes get a working link immediately on next page load; no DB backfill needed

summary: Rate Sheet after-hours rates now match the Market Rate Analysis card exactly — single source of truth

improved: lib/rateSheet.js now imports calcAfterHoursRates from lib/marketRates and uses it as the single source of truth for after-hours dispatches and additional hourly rates. The rate sheet preview, the View Rate Card document, the SPT export, and the Market Rate Analysis card's after-hours preview all read from the same function — they can no longer disagree

Mapping: rate sheet's 4 windows (Weekday 5-11pm, Weekend 7-5pm, Saturday 5-11pm, Graveyard & Sundays) map to calcAfterHoursRates' 3 categories (weekday_evening, weekend_day, graveyard). Saturday 5-11pm uses weekend_day rates since the previous admin defaults had it priced the same as weekend day
fixed: Rate Sheet after-hours rates were diverging from what reps saw in the Market Rate Analysis card. The card uses calcAfterHoursRates(onsite_additional) which scales the Ferrum baseline dispatch fees ($300/$285/$380) proportionally to the local market rate vs the $165 baseline (e.g. $300 × 152/165 ≈ $275 weekday dispatch). The rate sheet was using onsite_block_2hr × multiplier (introduced in v3.5.7) which produced different numbers ($432 weekday dispatch on the same quote). With market onsite_additional = $152 the new math now produces: Weekday $275 dispatch + $230/hr · Weekend $265 + $230/hr · Saturday $265 + $230/hr · Graveyard $350 + $265/hr — exactly matching what the rep sees on the card

Per-window 'Additional Hourly' rates in the rate sheet are now read from calcAfterHoursRates per window instead of using a single shared ahStdRate value. Previously Weekday/Weekend/Saturday all shared the exact same number, derived as remoteRate × 1.5. Now they pull weekday_evening_rate, weekend_day_rate, etc. from calcAfterHoursRates — which currently produces the same value for all three but means future changes to the formula (e.g. different multipliers for weekend vs weekday) only need to happen in one place

summary: Market Rate Analysis card — fixed the misleading 'SLIGHTLY HIGH' badge that was attached to the wrong rate

improved: MarketRateCard compact summary strip — the colored rating badge next to each rate now describes the displayed value's position vs the underlying market rate (FAIR / SLIGHTLY HIGH / HIGH / LOW). With no rep override the displayed value equals the market rate and shows FAIR. If a rep overrides upward, the badge shifts to SLIGHTLY HIGH or HIGH, warning that they're above market. If a rep overrides downward, LOW warns of underpricing. The badge is now an 'override sanity check' attached to the number it labels
fixed: MarketRateCard compact strip — fixed misleading rating placement. Previously getRating(BASE_RATES[key], val) compared Ferrum's published baseline (e.g. $330) to the market-adjusted rate (e.g. $288), producing a 'SLIGHTLY HIGH' label rendered next to the $288 figure — the label looked like it was describing $288 as slightly high, when really it was describing Ferrum's $330 as slightly high vs the $288 market. Reps were seeing 'Dispatch $288 SLIGHTLY HIGH' on a quote where they had accepted the recommended market rate and were correctly puzzled. The detail panel below the strip already had the rating in its proper column placement (next to 'FerrumIT Published'), so this fix only affects the compact strip

summary: After-hours dispatch fees now track market rates · FlexIT race condition fix · Flex Block replaces prepayment when selected

new: Rate Sheet now shows the on-site 2-hour dispatch block as an explicit line item (rate sourced from market analysis onsite_block_2hr) so reps and clients can see the dispatch block fee directly. Previously the rate sheet only showed onsite_additional ($/hr after 2hr min) and the 2hr block fee was implicit
improved: After-hours dispatch fees in the Rate Sheet are now derived from the market on-site 2-hour block × multiplier instead of flat admin defaults. Weekday 5-11pm, Weekend 7am-5pm, and Saturday 5-11pm dispatches now compute as onsite_block_2hr × oos_afterhours_mult_standard (1.5×). Graveyard / Sundays dispatch is onsite_block_2hr × oos_afterhours_mult_graveyard (2.0×). With a market on-site block of $288, after-hours dispatches are $432 and $576 respectively — previously the admin defaults of $300 / $285 / $285 / $380 were producing the absurd outcome where after-hours dispatch could cost LESS than a daytime dispatch in higher-rate markets

FlexIT On-Demand quote — the entire pricing pipeline (remoteRate, prepay calc, FlexTimeSelector, flex block calc, rate sheet preview, SPT export) now derives from a single effectiveRates value that prefers acceptedRates > marketAnalysis.rates. Previously these computations could fall back to oos_remote_rate or BASE_RATES if the marketAnalysis state was racing with onRatesAccepted

FlexIT On-Demand quote — billing summary restructured: when a flex block is selected it now appears as the single upfront line ('#1 — Flex Block — Nhrs pre-purchased — Due in full upon agreement signing'). The separate 'Initial Prepayment' row that previously rendered alongside the flex block is suppressed, since the flex block IS the upfront for that engagement

FlexIT On-Demand quote — when a flex block is selected, the left-column 'New Account Prepayment' callout, Prepayment Hours field, and Prepayment Amount field are all hidden and replaced with a clarifying note ('The {N}hr flex block is the upfront fee for this engagement — paid in full upon agreement signing. No separate initial prepayment is required when a flex block is selected.'). When no flex block is selected, those controls render as before

FlexIT On-Demand quote — the right-column KPI tile that previously always read 'Initial Prepayment' now flips to 'Flex Block (Nhr)' when one is selected, displaying the block price instead of the (now-zero) prepay amount

FlexIT SPT proposal export — the FlexIT Base Plan line item description now adapts to whether a flex block is present. When a flex block is selected it reads 'This engagement is initiated with the Flex Block pre-purchase listed below — that block fee is paid in full upon agreement signing... No separate hourly pre-payment is required.' instead of the contradictory 'A 2-hour labor pre-payment ($0.00) is required' that would have rendered before
fixed: FlexIT On-Demand quote — added an acceptedRates state separate from marketAnalysis. Previously the onRatesAccepted callback at line 530 used setMarketAnalysis(prev => prev ? {...prev, rates: {...prev.rates, ...rates}} : prev) which silently dropped accepted rates when prev was null. That happened whenever MarketRateCard's getRateSheet completed before handleZipChange's getOrAnalyzeMarket — the accepted overrides were applied to a null marketAnalysis (no-op), then handleZipChange finished and overwrote marketAnalysis with the original rates, losing the override entirely. Now acceptedRates is captured unconditionally and remoteRate/flexBlock/rateSheet all derive from a merged effectiveRates that prefers it

summary: Rate locking now actually preserves the locked rates — and Flex Time blocks honor them

improved: Market Rate Analysis card on saved/locked quotes now displays the locked-in rates instead of disappearing or showing blank values. When MarketRateCard finds an accepted rate sheet for a quote it now synthesizes a full analysis object from it (city, state, accepted_rates, market_zip) so the entire card body renders with the actual locked rates, ratings vs Ferrum baseline, and the 'Rate locked' label

Refresh button on the Market Rate Analysis card now requires explicit confirmation when the quote has accepted/locked rates — clicking it now warns 'This quote has locked-in rates. Refreshing will pull current market rates and replace what was locked. Continue?' Prevents accidentally wiping out negotiated rates by clicking refresh out of habit
fixed: Managed IT QuotePage — Flex Time block calculation now uses the accepted/locked market labor rate instead of the package default ($165) or the empty market_tiers row. New rate chain: live acceptedRates.remote_support → snapshotted acceptedRates → package default → market_tier (legacy) → 165 floor. So accepting $200 in the Market Rate Analysis card now produces flex blocks priced off $200 instead of $165
Managed IT QuotePage — FlexTimeSelector picker now also uses the accepted labor rate, so the per-block discount tiers shown to the rep match what will actually be calculated when they select one
Managed IT QuotePage — onRatesAccepted callback now captures the accepted rates into a new acceptedRates state (was previously discarding them and only keeping suggestedTier and pricing_multiplier)

Managed IT QuotePage — pricing_snapshot now persists acceptedRates alongside package/products/settings, so the locked labor rate survives quote reopen. On load, acceptedRates is restored from pricing_snapshot.acceptedRates immediately — the flex block picks them up before MarketRateCard re-fires onRatesAccepted from the saved rate sheet

Bundle quote — same acceptedRates flow as Managed IT (state, capture from MarketRateCard, persist in pricing_snapshot, restore on load, flex block uses the chain). Also fixes a separate bug: calcFlexBlock was being called with swapped arguments — calcFlexBlock(itResult.svcHrs, flexHours, settings) — so it was using svcHrs as the block size and flexHours as the labor rate. Now correctly calls calcFlexBlock(flexHours, flexBlockRate, settings)

Multi-Site quote — already pulls flex block rate from per-location market analysis (locationResults[0].analysis.rates.remote_support) so no fix needed there. FlexIT On-Demand also already uses marketAnalysis.rates.remote_support directly. Both inherit the MarketRateCard display improvements automatically

summary: FlexIT On-Demand — Market Rate Analysis card now shown on the quote (parity with IT/Voice/Bundle/Multi-Site)

new: Market Rate Analysis card added to the FlexIT quote, sitting directly above the Rate Card Preview in the right column. Reps can now see the full market analysis — city, state, market tier, pricing multiplier, per-rate ratings vs Ferrum baseline, refresh button, and rate-override controls — instead of just the computed rate sheet downstream of it

Wired the card's onRatesAccepted callback to FlexIT's marketAnalysis state — accepting overrides on a FlexIT quote updates the rate sheet immediately, and reopening a saved FlexIT quote with an accepted rate sheet restores those overrides automatically (same behavior as the other quote pages)
improved: FlexIT now matches the layout pattern used by the four other quote types — every quote page in Ferrum IQ shows the Market Rate Analysis card, the rate sheet preview, and the documents panel in the same right-column flow

summary: Removed legacy Area 2 / Metropolitan +30% surcharge — pricing now flows entirely from real-time market intelligence

improved: Rate Sheet generation in lib/rateSheet.js is now substantially simpler — buildRateSheet no longer applies a +30% surcharge multiplier to remote/onsite/dev/design/PC-setup rates or to fixed fees (same-day/next-day/cancellation/abort/after-hours dispatch). All rates flow straight from the market analysis (analysis.rates) and admin-configured fees (oos_*) without modification

rateSheet.meta no longer includes area2 or area2SurchargePct fields — saved snapshots from before v3.5.4 keep their old data but new rate sheets won't have these keys, and any consumer that read meta.area2 has been updated to stop checking
AREA2_CITIES constant and isArea2() function removed from lib/rateSheet.js — they're no longer needed and exporting them would be misleading
fixed: FlexIT On-Demand quote page — removed isArea2 import, area2Applied state derivation, the '⚠ Area 2 +30%' line under marketCity, the '(Area 2 +30%)' tooltip on the prepay auto-calc, the '· Area 2 +30% surcharge applies' span in the pricing summary, the yellow 'Area 2 surcharge applied' banner under the Rate Card Preview, and the '⚠ Metropolitan / Extended Area surcharge applies to all charges' notice in the FlexITPaymentModal. Removed the area2Applied prop from FlexITDocumentsPanel and FlexITPaymentModal signatures and from buildFlexITQuoteShape's input shape

RateSheetModal.js — removed the red 'AREA 2 +X%' badge from the modal header, the '· Area 2 +X% surcharge applied' suffix on the market detail badge, and the entire 'Metropolitan / Extended Area Coverage' yellow callout block listing AK / CA / HI / NV / NY / WA / Canada cities at the bottom of the rate sheet document

smartPricingTable.js — removed the 'Area 2 Market: Yes/No' SPT custom variable from the proposal payload (was being sent on every rate-sheet export), and removed area2Applied from buildFlexITQuoteShape's destructured props and returned shape

lib/sptFlexIT.js — removed the 'Metropolitan / Extended Area Coverage (See below)' placeholder line that was conditionally appended to the onsite rate card, removed the entire 'Area 2 Surcharge' HTML block (with the AK/CA/Canada/HI/NV/NY/WA city list and the conditional 'has been applied' notice) from the FlexIT proposal rate-card page, and removed the 'area-2' tag that was being added to FlexIT proposals when the surcharge applied

The oos_area2_surcharge pricing setting (defaulted 0.30 = 30%) is no longer read by any code path — the row can be safely deleted from pricing_settings via Admin → Pricing Settings, but leaving it in place is harmless

summary: FlexIT On-Demand — market intelligence now actually runs when no analysis exists for a ZIP

fixed: FlexIT On-Demand market analysis was a dead-end for unfamiliar ZIPs — handleZipChange ran a raw fetch against market_rate_analyses and silently failed when no row existed for the entered ZIP. Result: marketAnalysis stayed null, the Rate Card Preview kept showing 'Enter a ZIP code to see market-adjusted rates' even after a ZIP was entered, and SPT Create was blocked because the rate sheet builder requires a populated marketAnalysis

Replaced the raw supabase query with getOrAnalyzeMarket(zip, false, cityHint, stateHint, readOnly) — same helper the IT/Voice/Bundle pages use via MarketRateCard. It tries an exact-ZIP match, then a city/state match, then triggers an AI analysis to create a fresh market entry when none is found and the call isn't readOnly
City/state hints from the ZIP table are now passed into getOrAnalyzeMarket so the AI doesn't have to resolve unfamiliar ZIPs from scratch — speeds up first-time analyses for known cities
Existing FlexIT quotes load in readOnly mode (won't re-trigger AI on every reopen) — same pattern as MarketRateCard at quoteId-set

Rate Card Preview no longer shows the misleading 'Enter a ZIP code...' label when a ZIP IS entered. Now shows '⏳ Analyzing market rates for [ZIP]...' while AI is running, '✗ Market analysis unavailable: ...' on error, or 'No market analysis available yet for ZIP [ZIP]' if the call returned nothing

ZIP-driven market analysis now also kicks in when a ZIP comes in via HubSpot deal connect (the v3.5.1 pull-through path) — same handleZipChange entry point, so HubSpot connect on a fresh FlexIT quote populates the market analysis automatically

summary: VoiceQuotePage state recovery — restore fax picker cost+GM display and totals.onboarding write

fixed: VoiceQuotePage.js was inadvertently reverted to a pre-3.4.6 base in the v3.5.0 deploy because the build was generated from a stale snapshot of the project files. The library it depends on (voicePricing.js) and the Admin → Voice Fax Packages tab (AdminPage.js) were untouched and remained in their post-3.4.11 state, so the regression was localized to the page-level UI on Voice quotes only

Restored VoiceQuotePage.js from commit dea7732 (the post-3.4.8 state). This single restore brings back three features in one go: 3.4.5 waiver parity (status-aware International Dialing Waiver card with SENT/SIGNED/HUBSPOT badges), 3.4.6 fax picker cost + GM% strip (each fax option now shows sell · cost · GM% next to the suggested badge), and 3.4.8 totals.onboarding write on save (Voice quotes now write totals.onboarding alongside totals.nrc so the Saved Quotes onboarding column renders correctly)

Going forward, the deploy workflow (single zip with merged changelog inside) is locked into Claude's project memory — every future deploy will mirror the established repo structure and bundle the changelog entry in the same commit, preventing this kind of drift between sessions

summary: FlexIT On-Demand — HubSpot deal connect now pulls in address, contact, and ZIP

fixed: FlexIT On-Demand HubSpot deal connect was only pulling Deal ID, Deal URL, Deal Name, and Recipient Business Name — Business Address, Contact Name, Contact Email, and Client ZIP were never populated, blocking SPT proposal creation since the rate card requires the ZIP to compute market-adjusted rates

FlexITQuotePage onConnect now mirrors the Managed IT page handler: builds the full address from company.address + address2 + city + state + zip, sets Contact Name from contact.firstname + contact.lastname, sets Contact Email from contact.email, and pipes company.zip through handleZipChange so the market analysis fetch fires automatically
Proposal name auto-fills as 'FerrumIT On-Demand IT Support — [Company]' when not already set, matching the auto-fill pattern on other quote types
Falls back to extracting a business name from the deal title when no company is associated with the HubSpot deal

summary: FlexIT On-Demand → Smart Pricing Table — Create New now builds the full proposal

new: ⚡ Click Create New on a FlexIT quote and SPT now receives a fully-structured proposal — cover page, Service & Billing scope with the prepayment as a real line item, 7 standard T&M assumptions, market-adjusted Rate Card (Chicago $173.25/hr instead of the template's $165.00/hr boilerplate), Payment Schedule with Month to Month / Due Upon Receipt placeholders wired to SPT custom variables, and the full Acceptance Terms with all MSA exhibits
Flex Block pre-purchases come through as a second line item on the SPT scope when the rep selected a block — name reads 'Flex Block — Xhrs Pre-Purchase', priced at the discounted block rate

Area 2 surcharge handling — when the client ZIP is in a metro market, the inline +30% row is suppressed in the rate card (rates already include it) and a notice line is added so the client sees that the surcharge has been applied
Proposal name auto-fills as '[Client] — On-Demand IT Support (FIT-FLEX-####)' on FlexIT quotes (was incorrectly 'Managed IT Services' before)
improved: SPTConnect gained three optional props — quoteTypeLabel, defaultTags, customCreate — so any quote type can supply its own structured-proposal builder without forking the component. Voice / Bundle / Multi-Site keep their existing empty-proposal create flow unchanged for now

Create modal copy adapts to the customCreate path — button reads 'Build & Create Proposal in SPT' on FlexIT, and the helper text explains that a fully populated proposal is being built rather than an empty shell
